meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Professional occupations in art and culture
family.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
family.definition: This major group comprises professional occupations in art and culture. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 1. Occupations classified in TEER 1, as those in this group with a 1 as the second digit of their code, typically require a university degree (bachelor's, master's or doctorate) or the accumulation several years of experience and expertise in the subject matter knowledge in a related occupation found in TEER 2, when applicable (which includes occupations with a 2 as a second digit in their code).
family.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
family.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

units.title: Librarians
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.definition: Librarians select, develop, organize and maintain library collections and provide advisory services for users. They are employed in libraries or other establishments with library services throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Librarians select, develop, organize and maintain library collections and provide advisory services for users.
employment_requirements: A master's degree in library science or a master's degree in library and information studies or a master's degree from a program accredited by the American Library Association is required.
additional_information: Progression to library management positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishments with library services throughout the public and private sectors

core_competencies.competency: Critical Thinking, Decision Making and Problem Solving
core_competencies.statement: to evaluate the quality and relevance of resources, make informed and ethical choices in resource management and resolve user access challenges and research inquiries

core_competencies.competency: Learning and Teaching Strategies, and Instructing
core_competencies.statement: to guide users in conducting research using digital tools or accessing various library resources, and design educational programs and workshops that enhance users' information literacy
to read and understand complex written materials, including research articles, catalogue entries and reference documents, in order to assist patrons accurately and manage resources effectively
to systematically organize complex structured information, adapt classification schemes and efficiently retrieve and reorder resources so users can efficiently find what they need

to stay current with technological changes and updates in information management practices and adjust services to better meet user demands, such as by adapting to digital resources and online databases

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Library, Conservation, and Heritage
core_competencies.statement: to understand how to properly preserve and maintain properly valuable materials to protect a library's assets

units.title: Conservators and curators
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.definition: Conservators restore and conserve artifacts belonging to museums, galleries and owners of cultural property. Curators recommend the acquisition of museum artifacts and gallery works of art and research their artistic history. Conservators and curators are employed in museums, art galleries, archives, libraries, government agencies and universities. Conservators may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Conservators restore and conserve artifacts belonging to museums, galleries and owners of cultural property.
main_duties: Examine artifacts, determine their condition, suggest methods for treating them and recommend preventive conservation techniques to their owners
Restore and conserve paintings, photographs, books, papers, sculptures, furniture, pottery or other museum and art gallery artifacts as per particular field of specialization
Provide advice on display and storage of museum and gallery artifacts to ensure proper maintenance and preservation
Explore new conservation and restoration techniques
Provide consultation to museums, art galleries or private individuals
Supervise conservation technicians and other museum technicians.
employment_requirements: Conservators may be accredited by the Canadian Association of Professional Conservators.
Conservators require a master's degree in art conservation orcompletion of a two-year college program in conservation technology and several years of experience in conservation work.
additional_information: Progression to museum or art gallery management positions is possible with experience.
Conservators and curators may specialize in a particular type of collection or material, such as furniture, weapons, books, paintings and musical instruments.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Curators recommend the acquisition of museum artifacts and gallery works of art and research their artistic history.
main_duties: Recommend the acquisition of paintings, photographs, sculptures, documents and other museum and art gallery artifacts
Conduct research into objects' methods of construction techniques, structure and materials to understand its physical and chemical makeup
Develop storylines and themes and organize displays and exhibitions
Coordinate the storage of collections and the setting up of displays and exhibitions
Oversee the conservation, display and circulation of collections
Supervise curatorial assistants and other museum technicians.
employment_requirements: Curators require a master's or bachelor's degree in museology, art history or a field related to their specific area of work.
additional_information: Progression to museum or art gallery management positions is possible with experience.
Conservators and curators may specialize in a particular type of collection or material, such as furniture, weapons, books, paintings and musical instruments.

units.title: Archivists
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.definition: Archivists manage, process, store and disseminate information contained in an organization's archives. They acquire, store and research textual material, pictures, maps, architectural documents, electronic materials, films and videos, and sound recordings and multimedia materials. They are employed in archives, in the public and para-public sectors and in private sector organizations.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Archivists manage, process, store and disseminate information contained in an organization's archives. They acquire, store and research textual material, pictures, maps, architectural documents, electronic materials, films and videos, and sound recordings and multimedia materials.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree with a minor in archival studies ora university degree with a certificate in archival studies is required.
A master's degree in archival studies, archival studies and information science, library science or history may be required.
additional_information: Progression to archive management positions is possible with experience.

core_competencies.statement: to create clear and detailed documentation, reports and descriptive guides, as well as to effectively communicate archival practices and research findings to diverse audiences in writing

core_competencies.competency: Critical Thinking, Decision Making and Problem Solving
core_competencies.statement: to identify and address challenges related to the management of archival materials, such as deteriorating documents, incomplete records or complex storage requirements, and make informed decision

to adapt classification systems to various types of records and evolving archival standards in order to categorize materials, such as photographs, documents or electronic files, in a way that makes them accessible and usable for future reference
to accurately interpret materials (i.e., historical documents, correspondence and reports) for properly cataloguing and preservation, facilitating access and enhancing organizational knowledge
to meticulously document the specifics of each item, such as its condition, provenance and context, ensuring that records are preserved correctly and can be retrieved accurately

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Library, Conservation, and Heritage
core_competencies.statement: to understand how to manage textual materials and organize information to develop cataloguing practices that align with archival standards

units.title: Editors
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.minor_group_title: Writing, translating and related communications professionals
units.definition: Editors review, evaluate and edit manuscripts, articles, news reports and other material for publication, broadcast or interactive media and coordinate the activities of writers, journalists and other staff. They are employed by publishing firms, magazines, journals, newspapers, radio and television networks and stations, and by companies and government departments that produce publications such as newsletters, handbooks, manuals and Web sites. Editors may also work on a freelance basis.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Editors review, evaluate and edit manuscripts, articles, news reports and other material for publication, broadcast or interactive media and coordinate the activities of writers, journalists and other staff.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree in English, French, journalism or a related discipline is usually required.
Editors who specialize in a specific subject matter may be required to have training in that subject.
Membership in the Editors Association of Canada may be required.
Several years of experience in journalism, writing, publishing or a related field are usually required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory and management positions, such as editor-in-chief or managing editor, is possible with experience.
Editors may specialize in a particular subject area, such as news, sports or features, or in a particular type of publication, such as books, magazines, newspapers or manuals.

core_competencies.statement: to prioritize tasks efficiently, balance multiple projects, adhere to strict deadlines and adapt swiftly to changing editorial needs while maintaining high quality
to engage in constructive discussions to align with authors on content quality, deadlines and revisions while maintaining professional rapport and upholding publication objectives
to critically analyze, interpret and synthesize simple to complex texts, checking for clarity, coherence and adherence to editorial standards while providing insightful feedback for improvement
to convey comprehensive feedback, craft editorial notes, revise text and communicate at the appropriate tone  with writers and other stakeholders
to identify and correct errors in grammar, punctuation and formatting, ensuring the final content is polished, coherent and aligns with the intended style and voice
to understand language nuances, correct grammar and suggest writing changes to authors for clarity, storyline flow and to appeal to targeted audiences

units.title: Authors and writers (except technical)
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.minor_group_title: Writing, translating and related communications professionals
units.definition: Authors and writers plan, research and write books, scripts, storyboards, plays, essays, speeches and other non-journalistic articles for publication or presentation. They are employed by advertising agencies, governments, large corporations, private consulting firms, publishing firms, multimedia/new-media companies and other establishments, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Novelists, playwrights, script writers, poets and other creative writers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Novelists, playwrights, script writers, poets and other creative writers conceive and write novels, plays, scripts, poetry and other material for publication or presentation.
main_duties: Conceive and write novels, plays, scripts, poetry and other material for publication or presentation
May conduct research to establish factual content and to obtain other necessary information.
employment_requirements: Talent and ability, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work, are important hiring criteria.
Creative writing programs are offered by universities and colleges.
Membership in a guild or union related to the occupation may be required.
additional_information: Authors and writers may specialize in a particular subject or type of writing.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Content writers (non-technical) write e-copy, blog posts, e-books, podcasts and other forms of Web-based content.
main_duties: Write e-copy, blog posts, e-books, podcasts and other forms of Web-based content
Conduct research on a variety of topics to inform writing and may consult with clients to determine the goals of the writing.
employment_requirements: Talent and ability, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work, are important hiring criteria.
Membership in a guild or union related to the occupation may be required.
additional_information: Authors and writers may specialize in a particular subject or type of writing.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Copywriters write text for advertisements and commercials.
main_duties: Study and determine selling features of products and services and write text for advertisements and commercials.
employment_requirements: Copywriters usually require a university degree or college diploma in French, English, marketing, advertising or another discipline.
additional_information: Authors and writers may specialize in a particular subject or type of writing.

units.title: Technical writers
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.minor_group_title: Writing, translating and related communications professionals
units.definition: Technical writers write manuals and specifications for publication or presentation. They are employed by governments, large corporations, private consulting firms, publishing firms, and other establishments, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Technical writers write manuals and specifications for publication or presentation.
employment_requirements: A university degree in the area of specialization, such as computer science or engineering is usually required.

core_competencies.statement: to analyze complex technical documents, synthesize information from multiple sources and accurately interpret user needs to create clear and concise documentation
to effectively organize complex information, ensuring technical content is accessible and user-friendly for diverse audiences

to clearly and precisely convey complex technical information, ensuring documentation is easy to understand, accurately reflects the intended message and adheres to the appropriate technical and stylistic standards
to understand and interpret technical information and complex concepts, to grasp content from subject matter experts or technical sources

to ensure precision in technical content, spotting minor inconsistencies, errors, or ambiguities, in order to maintain clear, concise and accurate documentation that aligns with technical standards and user needs

to understand how the use of precise words can change the meaning and tone of a text, as well as bring greater clarity in writing instruction manuals, guides and other documents aimed at diverse audiences

units.title: Journalists
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.minor_group_title: Writing, translating and related communications professionals
units.definition: Journalists research, investigate, interpret and communicate news and public affairs through newspapers, television, radio and other media. They are employed by radio and television networks and stations, newspapers and magazines. Journalists may also work on a freelance basis.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Journalists research, investigate, interpret and communicate news and public affairs through newspapers, television, radio and other media.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in journalism or a related field such as communications is usually required.
additional_information: Experienced reporters may advance to editorial positions (print media) or become producers (broadcast media).
Journalists may specialize in print, broadcast or Webcast media, in particular issues such as political affairs or entertainment news, or in a particular geographic area.

core_competencies.statement: to interpret the emotions and motivations of diverse sources, ask relevant questions and engage effectively with different communities to gather accurate and nuanced information
to craft clear, engaging and informative content with accuracy and objectivity, capturing the essence of the news and conveying it effectively
to articulate complex ideas clearly and persuasively, communicate information effectively and engage audiences
to understand, interpret and synthesize complex information from diverse sources including documents, reports and interviews
to meticulously verify facts, adhere to journalistic standards and detect minor inconsistencies in information, ensuring accurate and trustworthy reporting
to understand journalistic standards and the impact of articles on public perception in order to critically assess the content, style and methods specific to the publication or broadcast

units.title: Translators, terminologists and interpreters
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.minor_group_title: Writing, translating and related communications professionals
units.definition: Translators translate written material from one language to another. Interpreters translate oral communication from one language to another during speeches, meetings, conferences, debates and conversation, or in court or before administrative tribunals. Terminologists conduct research to itemize terms connected with a certain field, define them and find equivalents in another language. Sign language interpreters use sign language to translate spoken language and vice versa during meetings, conversations, television programs or in other instances. Translators, terminologists and interpreters are employed by government, private translation and interpretation agencies, in-house translation services, large private corporations, international organizations and the media, or they may be self-employed. Sign language interpreters work in schools and courts, and for social service agencies, interpretation services, government services and television stations, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Translators and translator-revisers translate written material from one language to another.
main_duties: Translate a variety of written material such as correspondence, reports, legal documents, technical specifications and textbooks from one language to another, maintaining the content, context and style of the original material to the greatest extent possible
Localize software and accompanying technical documents to adapt them to another language and culture
Revise and correct translated material
May train and supervise other translators.
employment_requirements: A university degree in translation with a specialization in translation, interpretation or terminology in two languages including at least one of the two official languages ora university degree in a related discipline such as languages, linguistics, philology and courses in linguistic transfer and two years' experience as a full-time translator working in two languages, at least one of which is an official language orfive years of experience as a full-time translator working in two languages, at least one of which is an official language, are required.
Certification on dossier or by examination from the Canadian Translators, Terminologists and Interpreters Council (CTTIC) may be required for translators, terminologists and interpreters.
Fluency in three languages is usually required for translators or interpreters working in an international context.
Membership in a provincial or territorial association of translators, interpreters and terminologists may be required.
Use of professional titles may be regulated in some provinces.
additional_information: Translators, terminologists and interpreters specialize in two languages, such as French and English, the official languages of Canada. They may also specialize in another language and one of the official languages. The main areas of specialization include administrative, literary, scientific and technical translation. Interpreters may specialize in court, parliamentary or conference interpretation.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Terminologists conduct research to itemize terms connected with a certain field, define them and find equivalents in another language.
main_duties: Identify the terminology used in a field of activity

Conduct terminological research on a given subject or in response to inquiries for the preparation of glossaries, terminology banks, technological files, dictionaries, lexicons and resource centres, and add to terminological databases
Manage, update and circulate linguistic information collected from terminological databases
Provide consultative services to translators, interpreters and technical writers preparing legal, scientific or other documents that require specialized terminologies.
employment_requirements: A university degree in translation with a specialization in translation, interpretation or terminology in two languages including at least one of the two official languages ora university degree in a related discipline such as languages, linguistics, philology and courses in linguistic transfer and two years' experience as a full-time translator working in two languages, at least one of which is an official language orfive years of experience as a full-time translator working in two languages, at least one of which is an official language, are required.
Certification on dossier or by examination from the Canadian Translators, Terminologists and Interpreters Council (CTTIC) may be required for translators, terminologists and interpreters.
Membership in a provincial or territorial association of translators, interpreters and terminologists may be required.
additional_information: Translators, terminologists and interpreters specialize in two languages, such as French and English, the official languages of Canada. They may also specialize in another language and one of the official languages. The main areas of specialization include administrative, literary, scientific and technical translation. Interpreters may specialize in court, parliamentary or conference interpretation.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Interpreters translate oral communication from one language to another during speeches, meetings, conferences, debates and conversation, or in court or before administrative tribunals.
main_duties: Interpret oral communication from one language to another aloud or using electronic equipment, either simultaneously (as the speaker speaks), consecutively (after the speaker speaks) or whispered (speaking in a low whisper to one or two persons as the speaker is talking)
Provide interpretation services in court or before administrative tribunals
May interpret language for individuals and small groups travelling in Canada and abroad
May interpret for persons speaking an Aboriginal or foreign language in a variety of circumstances
May train other interpreters.
employment_requirements: A university degree in translation with a specialization in translation, interpretation or terminology in two languages including at least one of the two official languages ora university degree in a related discipline such as languages, linguistics, philology and courses in linguistic transfer and two years' experience as a full-time translator working in two languages, at least one of which is an official language orfive years of experience as a full-time translator working in two languages, at least one of which is an official language, are required.
Certification on dossier or by examination from the Canadian Translators, Terminologists and Interpreters Council (CTTIC) may be required for translators, terminologists and interpreters.
Fluency in three languages is usually required for translators or interpreters working in an international context.
Membership in a provincial or territorial association of translators, interpreters and terminologists may be required.
Use of professional titles may be regulated in some provinces.
additional_information: Translators, terminologists and interpreters specialize in two languages, such as French and English, the official languages of Canada. They may also specialize in another language and one of the official languages. The main areas of specialization include administrative, literary, scientific and technical translation. Interpreters may specialize in court, parliamentary or conference interpretation.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Sign language interpreters use sign language to translate spoken language and vice versa during meetings, conversations, television programs or in other instances.
main_duties: Translate sign language to a spoken language and vice versa either simultaneously or consecutively.
employment_requirements: Sign language interpreters require a college training program or a university certificate in sign language interpretation.
Sign language interpreters may require a certificate or certification evaluation in LSQ or ASL.
Membership in a provincial association of sign language interpreters may be required.
additional_information: Sign language interpreters work in French and Langue des signes québécoise (LSQ) or in English and American Sign Language (ASL).

units.title: Producers, directors, choreographers and related occupations
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.definition: Producers, directors, choreographers and professionals in related occupations oversee and control the technical and artistic aspects of film, television, video game, radio, dance and theatre productions. They are employed by film production companies, radio and television stations, video game companies, broadcast departments, advertising companies, sound recording studios, record production companies and dance companies. They may also be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Film, radio, television and video game producers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Producers, directors, choreographers and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Film, radio, television and video game producers oversee the various stages and scheduling involved in the production of presentations, motion pictures, television shows and radio programs.
main_duties: Plan, organize and control the various stages and scheduling involved in the production of presentations, motion pictures, television shows and radio programs; engage directors and other production personnel and supervise all technical personnel, and determine the treatment, scope and scheduling of production; maintain production archives, negotiate royalties, and may plan and control the budget pertaining to a particular production.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in the performing arts, broadcasting, journalism, business administration, theatre production or film studies and experience in a technical or production occupation in motion pictures, broadcasting or theatre are usually required.
Demonstrated creative ability is required for all occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Membership in a related guild or union may be required.

example_titles.job_title_text: technical producer - motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts
technical director - motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Producers, directors, choreographers and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Directors direct actors and technical crew throughout production and post-production to achieve desired presentation.
main_duties: Interpret scripts and direct the mise-en-scène; select and direct the actors, extras and technical crew; advise the cast and crew on the interpretation and delivery of the performance; direct rehearsals, filmings, broadcasts and performances; and confer with crew and specialists throughout production and post-production to achieve desired presentation.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in the performing arts, broadcasting, journalism, business administration, theatre production or film studies and experience in a technical or production occupation in motion pictures, broadcasting or theatre are usually required.
Demonstrated creative ability is required for all occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Membership in a related guild or union may be required.

example_titles.job_title_text: visual effects technical director - motion pictures

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Producers, directors, choreographers and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Choreographers create dances for film, theatre and television performances that convey stories, ideas and moods, and direct rehearsals for dancers to achieve desired interpretation.
main_duties: Create dances for film, theatre and television performances that convey stories, ideas and moods, and direct rehearsals for dancers to achieve desired interpretation.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in dance, or a period of study at a school of dance and extensive training with a dance company are usually required.
Demonstrated creative ability is required for all occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Membership in a related guild or union may be required.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Producers, directors, choreographers and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Art directors oversee and control artistic aspects of film, television, video game, radio, dance and theatre productions.
main_duties: Plan, organize and direct the artistic aspects of motion pictures, video games, stage productions and television shows by overseeing the design of sets, costumes, furnishings and props to create portrayals of period and setting.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in the performing arts, broadcasting, journalism, business administration, theatre production or film studies and experience in a technical or production occupation in motion pictures, broadcasting or theatre are usually required.
Demonstrated creative ability is required for all occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Membership in a related guild or union may be required.

example_titles.job_title_text: production designer - motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts
art director - motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Producers, directors, choreographers and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Film editors arrange motion picture film segments into sequences to achieve continuity and desired dramatic, comedic and thematic effects.
main_duties: Edit motion picture film and arrange film segments into sequences to achieve continuity and desired dramatic, comedic and thematic effects.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in film studies or cinematography and experience in camera operation or another technical occupation in video or motion picture production are usually required.
Demonstrated creative ability is required for all occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Membership in a related guild or union may be required.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Producers, directors, choreographers and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Record producers coordinate the production of musical recordings and manage technicians involved in duplicating, dubbing and mastering of recordings.
main_duties: Plan and coordinate the production of musical recordings, direct musicians and singers during rehearsals and recordings and manage technicians involved in duplicating, dubbing and mastering of recordings.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in music or the recording arts and experience in a technical or production occupation in the recording arts are usually required.
Demonstrated creative ability is required for all occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Membership in a related guild or union may be required.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Producers, directors, choreographers and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Directors of photography coordinate and direct the photography of motion pictures.
main_duties: Coordinate and direct the photography of motion pictures, supervise camera operators and other crews and determine lighting, lenses, camera angles, backgrounds and other elements to achieve desired visual effect.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in film studies or cinematography and experience in camera operation or another technical occupation in video or motion picture production are usually required.
Demonstrated creative ability is required for all occupations in this unit group.
additional_information: Membership in a related guild or union may be required.

units.title: Conductors, composers and arrangers
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.definition: Conductors, composers and arrangers conduct bands and orchestras, compose musical works and arrange instrumental and vocal compositions. They are employed by symphony and chamber orchestras, bands, choirs, sound recording companies, and orchestras for ballet and opera performances, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Conductors select and interpret musical works and lead bands, orchestras and choirs during rehearsals and performances.
main_duties: Select and interpret musical works and lead bands, orchestras and choirs during rehearsals and performances.
employment_requirements: A university degree in music or completion of a program at a music academy, conservatory or school of music is usually required.
A period of specialized study related to conducting, composing or arranging is usually required.
Experience as a musician is required.
Musical and creative talent and ability, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work, are important hiring criteria.
additional_information: Conductors, composers and arrangers usually specialize in a particular genre of music such as classical, country, jazz or popular.
workplaces_employers: Orchestras for ballet, opera or theatre performances

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Composers create musical compositions such as television and film scores, theme music, songs, symphonies and music for interactive products such as video games.
main_duties: Create musical compositions such as television and film scores, theme music, songs, symphonies and music for interactive products such as video games.
employment_requirements: A period of specialized study related to conducting, composing or arranging is usually required.
A university degree in music or completion of a program at a music academy, conservatory or school of music is usually required.
Experience as a musician is required.
Musical and creative talent and ability, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work, are important hiring criteria.
additional_information: Composers often work as arrangers.
Conductors, composers and arrangers usually specialize in a particular genre of music such as classical, country, jazz or popular.
workplaces_employers: Orchestras for ballet, opera or theatre performances

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Arrangers adapt and modify musical compositions to convey desired themes and to suit particular instrumental and vocal performances.
main_duties: Adapt and modify musical compositions to convey desired themes and to suit particular instrumental and vocal performances.
employment_requirements: A period of specialized study related to conducting, composing or arranging is usually required.
A university degree in music or completion of a program at a music academy, conservatory or school of music is usually required.
Experience as a musician is required.
Musical and creative talent and ability, as demonstrated by a portfolio of work, are important hiring criteria.
additional_information: Conductors, composers and arrangers usually specialize in a particular genre of music such as classical, country, jazz or popular.
workplaces_employers: Orchestras for ballet, opera or theatre performances

units.title: Musicians and singers
units.category: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.broad_category_title: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sport
units.major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.sub_major_group_title: Professional occupations in art and culture
units.definition: Musicians and singers perform with orchestras, choirs, opera companies and popular bands in establishments such as concert halls, lounges and theatres and in film, television and recording studios. This unit group also includes music teachers who usually teach in conservatories, academies and private homes.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sports (51, 52)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Musicians play one or more instruments in orchestras, opera companies and popular bands in establishments such as concert halls, lounges and theatres and in film, television and recording studios.
main_duties: Play one or more instruments as soloists or as members of a musical group before audiences or for recording purposes.
employment_requirements: Members of orchestras and other professional classical musicians and singers usually require a university degree in music.
Membership in a guild or union related to the occupation or type of performance may be required.
Musical talent and ability, as demonstrated during an audition, are important hiring criteria.
Musical training from a university or college or through private instruction is usually required
additional_information: Musicians and singers usually specialize in specific genres of music such as classical, country, ethnic, jazz, opera or popular music.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Singers sing musical arrangements in musicals, choirs, opera companies and popular music groups, in establishments such as concert halls, lounges and theaters, in film productions, on television and in recording studios.
main_duties: Sing musical arrangements as soloists or as members of vocal groups before audiences or for recording purposes.
employment_requirements: Members of orchestras and other professional classical musicians and singers usually require a university degree in music.
Membership in a guild or union related to the occupation or type of performance may be required.
Musical talent and ability, as demonstrated during an audition, are important hiring criteria.
Musical training from a university or college or through private instruction is usually required.
additional_information: Musicians and singers usually specialize in specific genres of music such as classical, country, ethnic, jazz, opera or popular music.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Teachers of music or voice teach technique, interpretation and music theory to students through private or group lessons.
main_duties: Teach technique, interpretation and music theory to students through private or group lessons.
employment_requirements: Musical training from a university or college or through private instruction is usually required.
Membership in a guild or union related to the occupation or type of performance may be required.
Musical talent and ability, as demonstrated during an audition, are important hiring criteria.

example_titles.job_title_text: music teacher - private, conservatory or studio lessons
